author: Antigravity Agent
funFacts: Woolly Mammoth possessed long, curved ivory tusks up to 14 feet in length, which it used to sweep snow off the grass it ate.
Woolly Mammoth coexisted with early humans, who hunted them for meat and used their bones and tusks to build shelters.
